The fix has been applied to the actual file on disk. Let me verify all the requirements from the fix instructions:

1. ✅ `padding: 6px 8px` (was `2px 6px`)
2. ✅ `min-width: 24px` added
3. ✅ `min-height: 24px` added
4. ✅ `font-size: 16px` kept
5. ✅ `line-height: 1` kept
6. ✅ `border: none` kept
7. ✅ `background: none` kept
8. ✅ `border-radius: 4px` kept
9. ✅ opacity/pointer-events behavior preserved

The fix matches the instructions exactly. The touch target is now at least 24x24px (with padding 6px 8px + font-size 16px line-height 1, the box is at least 24x24, actually likely ~28px tall x ~28px wide).

All requirements met. This is a CSS-only change as required. No scope expansion. The fix is correct and complete.

content.text: {"approved": true, "feedback": "The fix has been correctly applied to the `.row-menu-btn` rule in `src/app.css` (lines 313-323). The padding was changed from `2px 6px` to `6px 8px`, and `min-width: 24px` / `min-height: 24px` were added, satisfying the WCAG 2.5.8 (AA) 24x24 CSS px minimum target size. The visual glyph size is preserved (`font-size: 16px; line-height: 1` retained), and all other required properties are kept intact: `border: none`, `background: none`, `border-radius: 4px`, and the opacity/pointer-events behavior. The opacity/pointer-events cascade in the sibling rules (row hover, focus-within, focus-visible, aria-expanded) remains unchanged and consistent with the new sizing. This is a targeted, CSS-only change with no scope expansion.", "issues": []}
